title: Session 02 - Nouns, Articles and Orders
subject: english
description: A short original diagnostic-practice set for nouns, articles, countability and natural work-English collocations.

options: I work in food service sector.
I work in the food service sector.
I work in a food service sector.
questions.answer: I work in the food service sector.
questions.explanation: Use 'the' when referring to a recognised sector or industry.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: Most of my customers are Turkish ____.
questions.explanation: 'Most of my customers are...' needs a plural noun here.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct noun phrase: I want to create ____ regular ordering system.
questions.explanation: 'System' is a singular countable noun, so it needs an article.

questions.prompt: Which phrase is most natural in business English?
options: A restaurant gives an order to me.
A restaurant places an order with me.
A restaurant makes order on me.
questions.answer: A restaurant places an order with me.
questions.explanation: The natural collocation is 'place an order with someone'.

options: I earn a salary and commission.
I earn salary and a commissions.
I have a salary and commissions on payments.
questions.answer: I earn a salary and commission.
questions.explanation: 'Salary' is countable in this phrase; 'commission' is often used as an uncountable payment category.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: My commission is based ____ their monthly payments.
Correct this sentence: My customers mostly Turkish restaurants.
questions.answer: Most of my customers are Turkish restaurants.
questions.explanation: The sentence needs the verb 'are' and a more natural subject phrase.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence with two words: I work in ____ ____.
questions.explanation: Use 'the UK' with the article and capital letters.
A person is 'interested in' something. A thing can be 'interesting'.

questions.prompt: Write one natural sentence using: a regular weekly order.
questions.answer: A good customer places a regular weekly order with me.
questions.explanation: Use this as a model, but your own natural sentence can also be correct.
